description: P-401 (sync stdin), P-402 (trailing newline), P-403 (sort-as-data), P-501 (single-line comments) all applied without thought

scaleBars zero-range check was bypassed because I forced 0 into Math.max(...values,0)/Math.min(...values,0). For [5,5], range computed as 5 (max=5,min=0) not 0, so allEqual fallback never triggered. Test 'zero range → all bars length 1' caught it. Fix: compute actualMax/actualMin separately from scaleMin (which can clamp to 0).

Anchor scale at 0 for all-non-negative data (so 5 in [5,10] reads as half a bar, not almost-full). For mixed-sign data, use actualMin. Trade-off: violates data-vis purity (Tufte's 'don't lie about scale') but matches user intuition for typical use.

Cross-tool composition emerged: piped whoolen | whoograph produced a real chart of PAN's wordiest commands. focus-design.md = 8071 words. This is the Unix philosophy in action — small tools that compose. Strong promote candidate.
Malformed lines surface as marker pairs ({malformed: true}) rather than throwing. Caller can warn or filter. Robust-by-default rather than fail-fast.
